spec_match.rationale: The native implementation of translateStreaming yields the full text so far, but the callback appends chunks incrementally, causing duplication on native runs.

interactive.rationale: Provides good controls to play, stop, and select languages for the simulated and live captions.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns perfectly with the real-world live captioning use case of streaming translation.

edge_cases.rationale: Fails to handle the discrepancy between incremental fallback tokens and native full-text accumulated chunks.

references.rationale: References the correct ChromeStatus feature link.

design_system.rationale: Follows the layout, spacing, and typography conventions of the repository design system.

openQuestions.title: Fix translateStreaming chunk accumulation mismatch between native and fallback
openQuestions.detail: In native mode, translateStreaming() yields full accumulated text so far. However, the callback in streaming-captions/index.html uses `partial += chunk` which assumes incremental tokens, leading to duplicated output when native API is available. The fallback mode yields incremental tokens, creating a mismatch that needs to be unified.

summary: A well-designed concept demo for live captioning that has a severe API conformance bug: it treats native translateStreaming() chunks as incremental (appending them) when they are actually full accumulated text, leading to duplicated text on browsers supporting the native API.
